steps.title: Identify the architectural contrast
steps.content: The passage directly contrasts Calle Liceo's “traditional Mediterranean and Mexican styles” with the later Avenida Parque Mexico houses' “unadorned geometric forms,” showing how Barragán's approach evolved after his 1930s travels.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the answer
steps.content:
Answer C correctly identifies Calle Liceo as reflecting an earlier design approach that Barragán later abandoned.

The other three answers either place Calle Liceo within his later modernist phase (A and B) or overstate its influence throughout his career (D), contradicting the passage's explicit contrast between his earlier traditional style and later unadorned geometric forms.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
